releases.title: v2.10.0
releases.summary: This release upgrades the library from a single-format ownership model to a true multi-format ownership model. A single title can now carry more than one owned format without fragmenting into duplicate library records, and the main library add/edit flows, import flows, and format filtering all understand that ownership shape. Backward compatibility is preserved through a derived primary `format` value so the existing library surfaces can keep functioning while `owned_formats` becomes the canonical storage model.

releases.title: v2.9.9
releases.summary: This release moves External Logs from an env-only operator setup toward a real admin-managed control plane. Admin can now configure the common external log endpoint fields inside `Admin -> Integrations -> External Logs`, validate the running target, review the latest validation result, and understand whether the current runtime is using saved settings, env defaults, or an immutable env override. The existing fail-safe behavior remains intact: external export stays additive, collector failure stays non-blocking, and the primary DB-backed audit path remains durable.

releases.title: v2.9.8
releases.summary: This release ships two connected slices of hardening work. First, the app’s add/edit drawers, Help surfaces, and tabbed workflows were normalized onto calmer shared UI primitives so the product reads more consistently and accessibly across media, events, collectibles, help, and admin flows. Second, the runtime observability story was hardened for longer-lived operator use with clearer diagnostics, supported collector-path smokes, persistence rehearsals, and a repeatable observability release-evidence artifact that proves logs and metrics stay recoverable without turning collectors into a hidden runtime dependency.

releases.title: v2.9.6
releases.summary: This release introduces the first explicit `platform` versus `homelab` product-edition boundary inside the private repo. The backend now owns the active edition contract, the frontend shell and Help surface follow that server-owned shape, and platform-only control-plane routes no longer leak into homelab. The release also adds repeatable runtime smoke gates and CI enforcement so both sides of the split stay proven as the repo moves toward a later product/repo separation.

details.heading: Product edition boundary
bullets: Added a backend-owned edition contract with supported values:
Exposed the active `product_edition` through auth bootstrap so the frontend reads server-owned edition state instead of client-only assumptions.
Added a frontend shell composition boundary for homelab so platform control-plane surfaces are excluded as mounted routes and nav affordances, not just hidden late in rendering.

details.heading: Homelab shell and Help surface
bullets: Homelab Help now mounts a shared Help experience with:
Homelab does not mount:
Homelab shell/nav no longer exposes platform control-plane affordances such as:
Direct homelab navigation to platform-only dashboard tabs is redirected to homelab-safe destinations.

details.heading: Backend route-group separation
bullets: Split backend route mounting into shared versus platform-only groups so homelab no longer mounts platform-only APIs such as:
Kept shared homelab-safe surfaces mounted, including:

details.heading: Homelab product-shape tightening
bullets: Homelab shared auth/scope/library responses now keep:
While no longer surfacing:
Homelab registration is now invite-free after the first admin, removing the remaining dependency on the platform tenant-invite model for local account creation.
Homelab rejects generic authenticated space switching through shared scope APIs.
Homelab still allows meaningful library creation and library selection inside the single-context product shape.

details.heading: Runtime proof and CI enforcement
bullets: Added a repeatable homelab edition-boundary smoke gate to verify the shared mounted surfaces and unmounted platform-only APIs against a live homelab stack.
Added a matching platform edition-boundary smoke gate to verify that invite-based registration and the tenant/admin control plane remain mounted in platform.
Wired both edition gates into the blocking CI/release workflow so publish and preflight paths now depend on both sides of the product split.

releases.title: v2.9.5
releases.summary: This release expands the Playwright browser-regression layer from foundational coverage into broader product-critical workflows. It adds realistic browser protection for integrations persistence, barcode and CSV import paths, Help/Admin boundary behavior, and the Events/Collectibles surfaces, while keeping the suite deterministic through targeted helpers, cleanup paths, and job polling. The result is stronger browser confidence ahead of later frontend build-system work without bloating the suite into fragile snapshot noise.

details.heading: Critical browser flow expansion
bullets: Added Playwright coverage for Integrations save/update flows, including:
Added Playwright coverage for Import Media workflows:
Added Playwright coverage for Events and Collectibles:
Added browser-visible negative-path coverage for shell and admin/privacy boundaries:

details.heading: Browser harness ergonomics
bullets: Added reusable helpers for:
Kept test-created browser data cleaned up after runs so the suite remains reusable and deterministic.
Expanded roadmap/testing docs so the browser layer’s protected surfaces stay visible to maintainers.

details.heading: Browser-driven fixes and refinements
bullets: Fixed the admin integrations save query so the insert/update column list matches the values passed during real browser saves.
Fixed dashboard integration-section deep-link routing so supported sections like `metrics` and `logs` resolve correctly.
Hardened support/admin shell expectations with explicit browser coverage around redirect and surface-limit behavior.
Adjusted Events/Collectibles browser coverage to follow the real shell navigation and user-visible detail contracts instead of brittle direct-route or decorative overlay assumptions.

releases.title: v2.9.4
releases.summary: This release adds a first-class Playwright browser-regression layer to collectZ so release confidence no longer depends almost entirely on backend and API assertions. It ships a documented local and CI browser harness, a separate screenshot-capture path for support and docs use, and focused coverage for the most fragile auth, Help Center, Help Admin, and approved support-session workflows. While building that harness, the browser suite also exposed and drove fixes for request-linked support-session evidence, support-request status updates, and support-admin library switching during an active approved tenant session.

details.heading: Playwright browser-regression foundations
bullets: Added a root-level Playwright harness with documented local and CI invocation.
Added deterministic authenticated setup for browser tests, including seeded users and support fixtures where needed.
Added failure artifact capture for browser-regression runs so traces, screenshots, and videos are available when a flow breaks.
Added browser-regression coverage for:

details.heading: Screenshot-friendly support and docs captures
bullets: Added named screenshot capture mode separate from failure artifacts.
Added a separate manual-friendly browser-captures workflow so support and documentation images can be regenerated without coupling that work to the blocking browser-regression gate.
Added seeded support/admin capture paths that produce stable screenshots for:

details.heading: Browser-driven bug fixes
bullets: Fixed support-request status updates to avoid Postgres parameter-type ambiguity during browser-driven close/reopen flows.
Fixed request-linked support-session auth so active-session banner evidence keeps request and requester context.
Fixed support-admin library switching during active approved support sessions so scoped tenant-library changes work only inside the allowed support-session boundary.

details.heading: CI and packaging boundary
bullets: Added Playwright as tracked repo test infrastructure and as a CI release gate.
Kept Playwright out of the shipped backend/frontend runtime images.
Hardened CI artifact handling so saved Playwright auth bootstrap state stays outside uploaded artifact paths.
Changed the Playwright CI bypass token flow to use a per-run random token instead of a fixed shared value.

releases.title: v2.9.2
releases.summary: This release finishes the support workflow move from a loosely administrative action into a request-driven, explicitly approved support model. It ships the support foundations introduced during `2.9.1`, including the in-app Help center, Help Admin, release-note visibility, and support triage, then completes the `2.9.2` consent and session work by linking support sessions to approved requests, limiting `support_admin` tenant access to approved request context, and making the support UI easier to operate with clearer request identity, chat-style history, and compact operational controls.

details.heading: Help center and Help Admin foundations
bullets: Added the tabbed in-app Help experience with:
Added a dedicated Help Admin staff surface that unifies support reply and triage work instead of splitting it across separate admin views.
Added lightweight in-app release notes so users can see recent shipped changes without leaving the product.
Added queue badges and clearer support request identity with visible `SUP-######` request keys.

details.heading: Support requests, triage, and user-visible status
bullets: Added first-class support request creation and threaded replies.
Added staff triage controls for:
Added staff-only internal notes that save inline with the thread while remaining hidden from the requester.
Added support-side close and requester-side reopen actions so case lifecycle does not depend on one side remembering to resolve it.

details.heading: Consent and request-linked support access
bullets: Added explicit requester approval and revocation controls for support access on support requests.
Support access approval now creates clear in-thread system history instead of relying on out-of-band assumptions.
`support_admin` support-session starts are now request-linked and approval-gated:
Active support sessions now carry request identity so the authorized request stays visible during the session lifecycle.
`admin` retains the broader break-glass path as a separate operator capability.

details.heading: Help Admin operations UX
bullets: Help Admin now supports a denser operator workflow:
Guidance no longer mixes in redundant workload chrome for support staff.
Queue context now appears above the conversation thread so the thread column holds both operational context and the active case discussion.

releases.title: v2.9.0
releases.summary: This release completes the barcode-assisted entry milestone by turning barcode lookup and capture into a first-class workflow across the library and import surfaces. Shared barcode capture now supports direct camera or photo-assisted entry where browsers allow it, barcode lookup is media-type aware across books, audio, games, comics, movies, and TV, and direct cover-image attachment remains available without the abandoned OCR path. The release also hardens normalization, diagnostics, and result application against real-world UPC and ISBN cases encountered during milestone validation.

details.heading: Barcode-assisted entry completion
bullets: Added and hardened a shared barcode-assisted capture path for manual entry and import flows.
Barcode lookup is now available directly in the relevant library media types rather than being limited to a movie/TV-only path.
The add/edit flow supports camera or photo-assisted barcode capture where the browser can provide it, while preserving direct manual barcode entry as a fallback.

details.heading: Media-type-aware lookup and application
bullets: The shared barcode route now normalizes provider responses and enriches results by media type:
ISBN-scanned books now populate structured book fields like title, author, ISBN, publisher, and edition instead of collapsing metadata into a single title string.
TV season box set lookups now normalize to series-level TMDB searches instead of being pushed through the movie path.

details.heading: Normalization and diagnostics hardening
bullets: Barcode input is normalized more defensively across typed, pasted, and captured values.
Weak or noisy provider titles are cleaned before enrichment so packaging noise does not dominate search queries.
Representative real-world barcode cases were used to improve parsing for books, TV season box sets, and combo-pack movie releases.
Barcode lookup failures now preserve stage-aware diagnostics and request context so debugging stays practical when an upstream provider misbehaves.
Fixed the library/import button-handler bug that could send the click event object instead of the intended UPC.

details.heading: Capture and direct image attachment
bullets: Manual entry continues to support direct cover-image capture and upload where appropriate.
The OCR/vision identification path was removed from the product surface so the milestone now focuses on dependable barcode completion rather than unreliable image identification.

releases.title: v2.8.6
releases.summary: This release rounds out Events and Collectibles so they behave much more like the rest of the library. Shared object cards now align these surfaces with the main poster-style object language, drawers are read-first instead of implicitly editable, collectibles gained an optional artist field, and events now support their own images so they feel like first-class objects rather than lightweight utility records.

details.heading: Shared object treatment and interaction alignment
bullets: Introduced and reused a shared poster-style object card primitive so Events, Collectibles, and the main library object surfaces follow the same size, shape, badge placement, and hover-action language.
Fixed the shared hover action strip so invisible actions no longer capture clicks in hidden areas of the card.
Aligned card-click and row-click behavior so Events and Collectibles open detail drawers first, with edit as an explicit secondary action.

details.heading: Read-first detail drawers
bullets: Updated Events and Collectibles detail drawers so they present object information in a read-first label/value rhythm like other object detail views.
Removed the odd summary badge band from these view drawers.
Kept editing explicit:

details.heading: Events object improvements
bullets: Events now support first-class event images instead of only artifact-level images.
Event cards can render their own image treatment.
Event detail can show an image and provide a direct open-image action.
Event edit supports image URL, upload, device capture, and image removal.

details.heading: Collectibles object improvements
bullets: Added an optional `Artist` field to collectibles.
Collectible cards and detail drawers now surface artist information when present.
Collectibles continue to support first-class image handling and now fit more naturally beside Movies, Games, Books, Audio, and Comics.

releases.title: v2.8.5
releases.summary: This release simplifies the shell and Integrations surface so admin configuration reads more like supported product setup and less like a low-level control panel. The stale sidebar scope card is gone, Integrations now uses lighter horizontal tabs, and redundant or overly technical integration settings were removed or derived from known provider behavior.

details.heading: Navigation shell cleanup
bullets: Removed the old active-space/active-library card treatment from the sidebar shell.
Kept only the lighter library selector behavior where it remains meaningful for non-admin tenant use.

details.heading: Integrations surface simplification
bullets: Converted Integrations to the same calmer horizontal-tab language used elsewhere in admin surfaces.
Flattened the remaining heavy card framing so the active integration pane reads like the rest of Settings.
Moved `Metrics` and `External Logs` into first-class Integrations tabs instead of keeping them as a detached operator-export card.

details.heading: Integration config cleanup
bullets: Removed redundant integration toggles that no longer represented meaningful product choices:
Removed single-provider preset selectors where there was only one real supported source:
Removed editable low-level protocol fields such as custom key-header/query-param controls and dropped the unsupported `custom` preset path from the admin UI.
Shifted known provider protocol details back into backend-owned preset behavior for supported integrations.

details.heading: CWA, Plex, Comics, and Games cleanup
bullets: Derived `CWA Base URL` from the OPDS feed URL instead of requiring a second host field.
Removed CWA timeout as an admin-facing setting and normalized it to the backend default.
Removed Plex server-name editing from the admin UI.
Simplified Metron credentials so username and password sit together and the password field reads as a password instead of a token hybrid.
Tightened paired field layouts for CWA credentials, Games credentials, and Plex connection settings so related values sit together on desktop.
